title: Chat With AI: You're in Charge

modules.title: Meet Pixel: Your AI Pen Pal
modules.sub: Start a conversation and see how chatbots respond
modules.description: Learners are introduced to Pixel, a friendly AI character, and send their very first messages to kick off a pen-pal style exchange. Along the way they discover how a chatbot reads and replies to what they type, setting the stage for everything that follows.

modules.title: What Can Chatbots Actually Do?
modules.sub: Explore the real powers and limits of AI chat
modules.description: Learners put Pixel through its paces by trying out different kinds of requests — from answering trivia to giving advice — and sorting them into what chatbots handle well and what they struggle with. By the end, learners can explain in their own words what makes a chatbot useful and where its abilities stop.

modules.title: When Pixel Gets It Wrong
modules.sub: Spot mistakes and learn why AI isn't always right
modules.description: Learners read examples of Pixel making errors — giving outdated facts, misunderstanding a question, or making something up — and practice identifying at least two types of chatbot mistakes. They decide how they would check Pixel's answers in real life, building a habit of healthy skepticism toward AI responses.

modules.title: Write Your Own Chatbot Rules
modules.sub: Create a personal safety guide for using AI chat
modules.description: Learners reflect on everything they learned from their conversations with Pixel and draft their own set of personal rules for chatting with AI safely and wisely. They share and compare rules with the group, then choose one rule they are most proud of to keep as their own AI safety commitment.

synopsis: Step inside a story where you trade messages with an AI called Pixel and discover what chatbots can and cannot do. Every choice you make teaches you how to ask better questions and stay safe online.
rationale: Young children are already encountering chatbots in games and apps but have no framework for understanding what these tools are, how to prompt them effectively, or when not to trust their responses.
learning_outcome: Students can hold a purposeful conversation with a chatbot, identify at least two things chatbots get wrong, and write a personal safety rule for using AI chat tools.
source_signals: Meet Khanmigo: the student tutor AI being tested in school districts | 60 Minutes - CBS News
AI study buddy: how college students are using AI to learn smarter and faster - Microsoft
